1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
8	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible
11	
12	/-!
13	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
14	
15	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
17	`k`-vector space, via the pullback of the relative cotangent presheaf
18	`Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`.
19	
20	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
21	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
22	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
23	
24	The companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (rank =
25	relative dimension `n` from `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`) is
26	deferred to iter-129+ for blueprint-RHS-pinning work; not in this file.
27	
28	## Status (iter-129 fix-up: signature relaxed + renamed; iter-128 body preserved)
29	
30	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` (renamed from the
31	iter-128 `lieAlgebra`) is fully constructed (no `sorry`) via the
32	pullback-along-section bridge described in the declaration's
33	docstring. The signature has been relaxed from a hardcoded relative
34	dimension `1` to a free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
35	binder, so the same definition serves any group scheme (including the
36	abelian-variety consumer `rigidity_over_kbar` of arbitrary relative
37	dimension `g`). The iter-129+ rank lemma
38	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` lives in a follow-up declaration
39	(not in this file).
40	
41	## References
42	
43	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
44	- Project infrastructure consumed:
45	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`
46	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
47	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
48	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
49	-/
50	
51	set_option autoImplicit false
52	
53	universe u
54	
55	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
56	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
57	
58	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
59	
60	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
61	
62	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
63	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
64	`ModuleCat k`).
65	
66	Mathematically: `η_G^* Ω_{G/k}`, the pullback of the relative cotangent
67	sheaf along the identity section. By smoothness of `G` at `η_G` this is
68	a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank equal to the relative
69	dimension of `G.hom`; the iter-129+ companion rank lemma
70	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (in a follow-up declaration) pins
71	the rank to `n` from the `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
72	
73	The Lie algebra `𝔤` of `G` (the tangent space at the identity) is the
74	`k`-linear dual of this module; downstream consumers that need `𝔤` may
75	take `Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`. We do not dualise here
76	because the rigidity argument is symmetric under dualisation (only the
77	finite-rank-free claim is consumed) and the un-dualised form is the
78	direct output of the pullback-along-section construction.
79	
80	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
81	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument.
82	
83	**Construction (iter-128 prover lane; signature relaxed iter-129).**
84	The body uses the pullback-along-section bridge:
85	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
86	   a scheme morphism `η_G.left : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
87	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
88	2. Sections on the top open give a commutative ring map
89	   `Γ(G.left, ⊤) ⟶ Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the canonical iso
90	   `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) yields
91	   `ψ : Γ(G.left, ⊤) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
92	3. `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom` evaluated at the top open
93	   yields a `Γ(G.left, ⊤)`-module, the module of global relative
94	   differentials of `G/k`.
95	4. Extension of scalars along `ψ` to `k` (i.e. tensoring with `k`
96	   over `Γ(G.left, ⊤)`) gives the cotangent space at the identity
97	   section as a `k`-module.
98	
99	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The structural
100	properties (`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`, rank `= n`) are
101	content for the iter-129+ rank lemma. -/
102	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
103	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
104	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
105	    ModuleCat k :=
106	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
107	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
108	  -- Ring map `Γ(G.left, ⊤) ⟶ k` from sections of the identity section,
109	  -- composed with the `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` adjunction iso.
110	  let ψ : Γ(G.left, ⊤) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
111	    ηleft.appTop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
112	  -- Relative cotangent presheaf evaluated at the top open of `G`.
113	  let M := Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom
114	  -- Extend scalars from `Γ(G.left, ⊤)` to `k` along `ψ`; this is the
115	  -- global-sections form of `η_G^* Ω_{G/k}`.
116	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj (M.obj (op ⊤))
117	
118	end AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj

40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} — but for the \emph{colimit-source} ring $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$, not for $\struct{S}(U)$. Transferring to the classical $A = \struct{S}(U)$ source on an affine chart is the bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of $X$ admits an affine chart $V \to U$ over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of the prescribed rank, with $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free (algebra-K\"ahler form)]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
53	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exist an affine open $U \subseteq S$, an affine open $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \subseteq f^{-1}(U)$, such that the K\"ahler differential module
54	  \[
55	    \Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) \,/\, \Gamma(S, U)}
56	  \]
57	  (over the appLE algebra structure $\Gamma(S, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)$ induced by $f$) is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$.
58	\end{theorem}
59	
60	\begin{proof}
61	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
62	  \leanok
63	  Fix $x \in X$. We use the abbreviation $A := \Gamma(S, U)$ and $B := \Gamma(X, V)$ for the section rings on the affine chart we will produce; the algebra map $A \to B$ is the appLE map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}$ induced by $f$.
64	
65	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at $x$.} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
66	  \[
67	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
68	  \]
69	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ into: for every $x \in X$, there exist affine opens $U \subseteq S$ and $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \leq f^{-1}U$, such that the induced algebra map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
70	
71	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
72	  \[
73	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
74	  \]
75	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$ from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
76	
77	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
78	  \[
79	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
80	  \]
81	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~$B$~$\Omega_{B/A}$ from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$.
82	
83	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
84	  \[
85	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
86	  \]
87	  (same file as Step 3) gives $\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n$ under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
88	
89	  \emph{Step 4.5: discharge the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ side condition.} The chart witness $x \in V$ shows that the open $V \subseteq X$ is non-empty as a set, so its structure-sheaf section ring $B = \Gamma(X, V) = \struct{X}(V)$ is non-zero by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (the structure sheaf of a scheme has \texttt{Nontrivial} sections on a non-empty open). This supplies the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ hypothesis required by Step 4.
90	
91	  Combining Steps 1--4.5, the chart $(U, V, e)$ produced by Step 1 satisfies $x \in V$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,U$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,V$, and under the appLE algebra structure $A = \Gamma(S, U) \to B = \Gamma(X, V)$, the K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is a free $B$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the statement follows.
92	
93	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
94	
95	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
96	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
97	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
98	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
99	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
100	  and \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (Step 4.5).
101	\end{proof}

151	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
152	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
153	
154	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
155	
156	The reverse implication
157	\[
158	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
159	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
160	\]
161	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
162	
163	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
164	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
165	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
166	\end{remark}
167	
168	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
169	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
170	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
171	  \[
172	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
173	  \]
174	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
175	  \begin{itemize}
176	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
177	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
178	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
179	  \end{itemize}
180	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
181	\end{remark}
182	
183	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
184	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
185	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
186	\end{remark}
187	
188	\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}
189	
190	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
191	
192	\begin{itemize}
193	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
194	
195	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
196	  \[
197	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
198	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
199	  \]
200	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
201	
202	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
203	
204	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
205	  \[
206	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
207	  \]
208	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
209	\end{itemize}

\begin{lemma}[(i.a) Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme]
  \label{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}
  % Lean signature stub (pinned post-iter-129 fixup; relaxed from the
  % iter-128 hardcoded rel-dim-1 form so the same declaration serves
  % consumers of arbitrary relative dimension, e.g. \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
  % applied to an abelian variety of dimension $g$):
  %   noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
  %       [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] : ModuleCat k
  The pullback along the identity section $\eta_G$ of the relative cotangent presheaf,
  \[
    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;:=\; \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k},
  \]
  is a finitely generated free $k$-module. We call $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ the \emph{cotangent space of $G$ at the identity}. The \emph{Lie algebra of $G$} as a $k$-vector space is recovered as the $k$-linear dual $\mathfrak g := \mathfrak g^{\vee\vee} = \mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k)$ (the bracket structure is not used in the rigidity argument and is not packaged here).

  \emph{Lean encoding note (iter-129 dualisation pinning).} The Lean declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} returns a \texttt{ModuleCat k} which is the \emph{un-dualised} cotangent $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ — the direct output of the pullback-along-section construction described in the proof, with no internal \texttt{Module.Dual} hop. Downstream consumers that need the tangent $\mathfrak g$ take \texttt{Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)} externally. This convention pins what was left open in the iter-128 encoding note: the rigidity argument is symmetric under dualisation (only the finite-rank-free claim is consumed), so the un-dualised form is preferred as it matches the body construction without an extra contravariant hop. The declaration embeds into Mathlib's \texttt{ModuleCat} infrastructure and supplies the \texttt{Module.Free k} / \texttt{Module.Finite k} typeclasses needed for the rank lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} via the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  The identity section $\eta_G$ is a canonical $k$-rational point of $G$ supplied by the $\GrpObj$ structure. The iter-128 Lean construction realises $\eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ as the extension of scalars to $k$, along the ring map $\psi \colon \Gamma(G, \mathcal O_{G}) \to k$ induced by $\eta_G$ on global sections, of the global-sections module of the relative cotangent presheaf:
  \[
    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;\cong\; k \,\otimes_{\Gamma(G,\,\mathcal O_{G})}\, \bigl(\Omega_{G/k}\bigr)(G).
  \]
  Equivalently — and this is the content of the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} below — this $k$-module is canonically isomorphic to the cotangent space $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G} / \mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ of the local ring $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ at the identity (as a $k$-vector space via the residue map). Smoothness of $G$ at $\eta_G$ makes that local ring regular (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}), and for a regular local ring the cotangent module $\mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^{2}$ — provided by Mathlib as \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (defined as \texttt{(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}) — is a finitely generated free module over the residue field, of rank equal to the Krull dimension. Combining with the bridge lemma yields the finitely-generated-free claim for $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$; the precise rank value is the content of \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[(i.a-bridge) Bridge to the local-ring cotangent space at the identity]
  \label{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_iso_localRingCotangent}
  % Lean signature stub (iter-130+ build target; bridges the iter-128 body
  % construction to the local-ring cotangent space supplied by Mathlib):
  %   noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_iso_localRingCotangent
  %       (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
  %       [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
  %       cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G ≅
  %         ModuleCat.of k (Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace
  %           (R := Scheme.stalk G.left (η_G.left.base ⟨_, trivial⟩)))
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
  Let $G$ be as in \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}, and let $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ denote the local ring of $G$ at the identity (the stalk along $\eta_G : \Spec k \to G$), with maximal ideal $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}$ and residue map $\pi \colon \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} \to k$. Then there is a canonical $k$-linear isomorphism
  \[
    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}
    \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
    \mathfrak m_{\eta_G} \big/ \mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}.
  \]
  Concretely, the right-hand side is \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} applied to the local ring $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ (as a module over its residue field, identified with $k$ via the $\GrpObj$-supplied identity section); the left-hand side is the iter-128 evaluate-then-extend-scalars Lean body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
  The bridge is the composition of two standard identifications (both at the level of $k$-modules):

  \emph{Step 1: from global differentials to the differential module of the local stalk-residue map.} The iter-128 body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} extends scalars of $(\Omega_{G/k})(G)$ along the ring map $\psi \colon \Gamma(G, \mathcal O_{G}) \to k$ induced by $\eta_G$ on global sections. Factor $\psi$ through the localisation at the identity stalk and the residue map,
  \[
    \Gamma(G, \mathcal O_{G}) \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{loc}\;}\; \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} \;\xrightarrow{\;\pi\;}\; k.
  \]
  Localisation kills relative differentials (\texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential}, used already as \cref{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton} of Differentials.tex), so extension of scalars along the localisation step preserves $\Omega$ as the Kähler differential module $\Omega_{\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}/k}$. The full extension along $\psi$ is therefore $k \otimes_{\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}} \Omega_{\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}/k}$.

  \emph{Step 2: from the Kähler module of a local $k$-algebra with residue field $k$ to $\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}$.} For a local ring $(R, \mathfrak m, k)$ that is a $k$-algebra with residue map $\pi \colon R \to k$ a $k$-algebra retraction (here supplied by the identity section $\eta_G$ producing $R = \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ over $k$ with residue field canonically $k$), the standard identification
  \[
    k \,\otimes_{R}\, \Omega_{R/k} \;\;\cong\;\; \mathfrak m \big/ \mathfrak m^{2}
  \]
  holds via the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon R \to \Omega_{R/k}$ restricted to $\mathfrak m$ (the kernel of $\pi$). This is Stacks Tag~02G1 (or Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~II.8.7 in the Noetherian case) and is in Mathlib at the ring level via \texttt{Algebra.Extension.CotangentSpace} infrastructure (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Basic}); the present blueprint statement chooses the local-ring framing \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}, the abbreviation \texttt{(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent}) as the cleanest container for the rank step in \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}.

  Composing Steps 1 and 2 yields the displayed isomorphism. No regularity input is needed for the bridge itself: the bridge is a tautological identification of two constructions of the same $k$-module, valid for any group scheme with an identity section in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$. Regularity enters only at the rank-pinning step (\cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}).
\end{proof}

  \label{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq}

  %       [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
  %       Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G) = n
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}
  \notready
  In the setting of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}, with $[\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,n\,G.\mathtt{hom}]$ supplying the relative dimension parameter,
  \[
    \finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} \;=\; n,
  \]
  and consequently (since rank is preserved under $k$-linear duality on finitely-generated free modules) $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g = n$ as well.

  \emph{Lean encoding note (iter-128 per \texttt{strategy-critic-iter128}; iter-129 rename).} The right-hand side is the relative-dimension parameter $n$ from the \texttt{[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]} instance, NOT a bare $\dim G$ (which has no canonical Lean function on \texttt{Over (Spec k)}). The mathematical content is unchanged because for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, the relative dimension equals the dimension of the cotangent space at the identity equals what informal prose calls $\dim G$. The Lean target name is \texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq} (iter-129 rename, replacing the iter-128 placeholder \texttt{lieAlgebra\_finrank\_eq\_dim}).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}
  Combine four standard pieces.

  \emph{Step 1: bridge to the local-ring cotangent space.} By \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}, the iter-128 evaluate-then-extend-scalars body of \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} is $k$-linearly isomorphic to $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G} / \mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$, the local-ring cotangent space of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$. The Mathlib container for the RHS is \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent}), and rank is preserved across the isomorphism: it suffices to compute $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2} = n$.

  \emph{Step 2: regularity of the local ring at the identity.} Smoothness of $f \colon G \to \Spec k$ at the closed point $\eta_G$ implies that $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ is a regular local ring (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}; Stacks Tag~00TR / Hartshorne~II.10.3.B). The Krull dimension of $\mathcal O_{G, \eta_G}$ equals the relative dimension $n$ of $f$ at $\eta_G$: this is the smooth-implies-rel-dim-equals-Krull-dim-of-the-stalk fact (Stacks Tag~02G1 / Hartshorne~II.10.4 for the smooth case). Both facts package together in the Mathlib \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ class via the local-ring instance attached to a closed point of a smooth scheme.

  \emph{Step 3: cotangent rank equals Krull dimension for a regular local ring.} For a regular local ring $(R, \mathfrak m)$, the cotangent space \texttt{Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace}~$R$ is finitely generated free over the residue field with rank equal to the Krull dimension; this is essentially the defining property of regularity. Combined with Step 2: $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2} = \dim \mathcal O_{G, \eta_G} = n$.

  \emph{Step 4: cross-check via the affine-chart Kähler rank.} The alternative route through the project's already-formalised forward direction of the smoothness criterion (\cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}, i.e.\ \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}) reaches the same rank: on a standard-smooth affine chart $(U, V)$ around $\eta_G$, the algebra-Kähler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of rank $n$ by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}); extension of scalars along the residue map at $\eta_G$ preserves the rank to give $\finrank_{k}\,\mathfrak g^{\vee} = n$. \end{proof}
